title: OpenGalois Proof-Carrying Certificate (Facts/Rules Model)
description: Proof-carrying, glass-box certificate for polynomials over Q of degree 1..5. Proof obligations are expressed as a fact graph: each node asserts a typed fact (claim) and references a rule (verification procedure) plus premises and optional evidence.

rational_canonical.description: Canonical Q element: 0 | nonzero integer | reduced fraction p/q with q>=2, gcd(|p|,q)=1, q>0. Disallows '-0' and any denominator 1.

rational_nonzero_canonical.description: Canonical nonzero Q element: nonzero integer | reduced fraction p/q with q>=2, gcd(|p|,q)=1, q>0. Disallows '-0' and any denominator 1.

object_ref.description: Reference to '$input' or a key in the top-level 'objects' map.

fact_ref.description: Reference to a fact node id.

id.description: Stable identifier for this proved fact node.

pred.description: Predicate symbol (from the ruleset's fact catalog).

args.description: Ordered predicate arguments as references to '$input' or objects in the object store. Arity and expected kinds are defined by the ruleset's fact catalog.

rule.description: Rule identifier (stable, versioned) from the referenced ruleset.

premises.description: List of fact node ids that this node depends on (normative). Interpreted by the rule checker.

evidence.description: Optional rule-defined evidence payload (normative if required by the rule).

statement.description: Optional human-readable statement for pedagogy/audit; non-normative.

data.description: Optional computed data for pedagogy/audit; non-normative.

ruleset_id.description: Ruleset/profile identifier that defines the allowed predicates, rules, and lookups.

hash_scope.description: Hash computed over the canonical JCS representation of objects['$input'].

objects.description: Object store for sharing computed mathematical objects across proof steps. Keys are stable IDs.

facts.description: A list of proved fact nodes. Nodes reference each other via premises (by id).

goals.description: Optional list of fact node ids that represent the claimed final conclusions (normative if present).

hash_scope.description: Hash computed over the canonical normative certificate view, excluding integrity and all non-normative fields.

summary.description: Optional non-normative summary for UX. Verifiers MUST rely on proof + recomputation, not on this section.
